sources: Fostering Undergraduate Talent by Unlocking Resources (FUTURE) Act

2026.methodology: The 2026 figures are derived from the FY 2026 President's Budget Summary which proposes a reduction in discretionary authority to $66.7 billion. Total spending (Outlays) is projected higher than new income due to the continued disbursement of prior-year obligations and multi-year grant cycles. The breakdown reflects the administration's policy shift toward consolidating disparate programs into flexible block grants and prioritizing educational choice programs over centralized federal mandates.

sources: Individuals with Disabilities Education Act (IDEA) Recurring Funds

higher_education_and_student_aid.name: Higher Education and Federal Student Aid (Pell Grants & Loans)

elementary_and_secondary_education.name: Elementary and Secondary Education (Title I & School Support)

2025.methodology: Income reflects total Enacted Budget Authority for FY 2025. Spending represents Total Net Outlays as recorded by the Department of the Treasury's Monthly Treasury Statement (MTS). The gap between income and spending is primarily due to the ongoing disbursement of carryover funds from pandemic-era relief programs (ESSER) and upward technical adjustments in student loan subsidy costs.

sources: Individuals with Disabilities Education Act (IDEA) Allocations

elementary_secondary_education.name: Elementary and Secondary Education (Title I & School Support)

covid_recovery_grants.name: COVID-19 Relief and Pandemic Recovery Grants

2024.methodology: Total income is based on the enacted Congressional appropriations for FY2024. Spending reflects 'Net Outlays' as reported by USAFacts and the Department's financial statements, which includes actual payments made during the fiscal year. The discrepancy between income (appropriations) and spending (outlays) is attributed to the distribution of multi-year COVID-19 relief funds (ESSER) and student loan accounting adjustments.
